mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to precede negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal generates false positives in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 225, rising price levels erode real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 58% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates a shift toward consumer-driven demand that disproportionately benefits large-cap equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a broad-based contraction in economic output, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST fail to precede negative 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal produces false negatives in > 65% of triggered instances.
